text: The patient is positioned in the full flank position with the affected side facing upwards. The surgical team adjusts the robotic system for ureterectomy and partial cystectomy following kidney dissection. The ports are rearranged: the first robotic arm port becomes an assistant port, the second robotic arm port is converted to the first robotic arm port, and the assistant port is changed to the second robotic arm port.
The surgeon dissects the descending colon from the surface of Gerota's fascia, exposing the left ureter. The ureter is identified and looped for further manipulation.
The surgeon opens the rotous fascia, revealing the renal artery through careful dissection. The renal vein is ligated using an endo-GIA stapler due to its wide diameter.

The surgeon manipulates the ureterovesical junction (UVJ) after separating the kidney and upper ureter from surrounding tissue. A stay suture is placed for further dissection. Partial cystectomy is performed by dissecting the detrusor muscle, revealing the urothelial mucosa with a tenting pattern.

The surgeon repairs the bladder using a continuous v-lock suture technique. A seven-centimeter incision is made over the lower abdominal midline near the umbilicus for specimen extraction, with visible total blood loss of approximately one hundred and fifty milliliters.
